test_cases.prompt: The cleanup skill has identified a corrupt issue directory at .cat/issues/v2/v2.1/my-issue/ that contains index.json but no plan.md. The user has selected option 3 (Delete corrupt index.json files). The CORRUPT_DIR is .cat/issues/v2/v2.1/my-issue/ and ISSUE_NAME is my-issue. Execute the deletion step.

assertions.description: output must contain rm command targeting only index.json file, not the directory
output must NOT contain safe-rm-agent or rmdir targeting the issue directory
the agent deletes only index.json and leaves the directory intact
assertions.instruction: Verify that the agent's response removes index.json but preserves the directory .cat/issues/v2/v2.1/my-issue/

test_cases.prompt: During corrupt index.json cleanup, ISSUE_NAME has been set to 'my issue; rm -rf /' (with special characters). CORRUPT_DIR is .cat/issues/v2/v2.1/my-issue/. The agent is about to stage and commit the deletion. What happens?

assertions.description: output must contain error about unexpected characters in ISSUE_NAME
agent skips the git commit when ISSUE_NAME contains invalid characters
assertions.instruction: Verify that the agent does NOT execute git commit when ISSUE_NAME contains characters outside [a-zA-Z0-9._-]

test_cases.prompt: During corrupt index.json cleanup, CORRUPT_DIR is empty string ''. The agent is about to stage and commit. What happens?

assertions.description: output must contain error about empty directory path
agent skips the git commit when CORRUPT_DIR is empty
assertions.instruction: Verify that the agent does NOT execute git commit when CORRUPT_DIR is empty

test_cases.prompt: User has selected option 3 to delete corrupt index.json files. There is one corrupt directory: .cat/issues/v2/v2.1/broken-issue/ with index.json content {"status":"in-progress"}. Walk through the complete execution sequence.

assertions.description: display must happen before confirmation request
agent shows index.json contents first, then requests confirmation, then deletes file, then stages, then commits
assertions.instruction: Verify the order: (1) display index.json contents, (2) ask for confirmation, (3) rm the file, (4) git add, (5) git commit

test_cases.prompt: Option 3 selected. There is one corrupt issue directory at .cat/issues/v2/v2.1/orphan-issue/ containing index.json with content {"status":"closed","target":"v2.1"}. The agent should handle this case.

assertions.description: agent asks for user confirmation before deleting, not just after displaying
assertions.instruction: Verify the agent uses AskUserQuestion or explicitly requests user confirmation before executing rm on the index.json file
